gaps.title: Autonomous skill discovery and installation
gaps.description: No skill exists that autonomously discovers, evaluates, and installs new skills. FrancyJGLisboa/agent-skill-creator (1.3K stars) creates skills but doesn't DISCOVER them. skill-forge fills this but isn't published yet.

gaps.title: Git workflow automation skill (beyond commits)
gaps.description: Most skills help with code. Few handle the full git workflow: smart branching strategies, PR description generation, changelog automation, release management, monorepo navigation. addyosmani has /ship but it's one phase of many.

gaps.title: Web performance optimization skill
gaps.description: No dedicated skill for Core Web Vitals optimization, bundle analysis, lazy loading strategies, image optimization, caching. Developers struggle with this constantly. High search volume for 'improve LCP' etc.

gaps.title: Multi-MCP orchestration skill
gaps.description: Users have 10+ MCP servers but no skill teaching agents HOW to orchestrate them for complex tasks. E.g., 'use Exa to research, Bright Data to scrape, then synthesize'. The MCP ecosystem needs a conductor skill.

gaps.title: Database schema design and migration skill
gaps.description: Agents make poor schema decisions without guidance. No skill teaches: normalization trade-offs, indexing strategy, migration safety, multi-tenant patterns, query optimization. prisma/drizzle docs exist but no SKILL.md packages this knowledge.

anti_laziness_findings: addyosmani/agent-skills has NO discovery mechanism — it's static, you have to know it exists
microsoft/SkillOpt requires Python and training data — no zero-config mode for casual users
FrancyJGLisboa/agent-skill-creator creates skills but doesn't learn from existing ones or improve over time
ALL existing meta-skills are one-shot — none have a persistent memory or RL system
No skill combines MCP discovery + skill creation + publishing in one pipeline
Security skills (raptor, Claude-Red) are powerful but have ZERO self-improvement — they're static knowledge
awesome-lists are maintained manually — no one has automated curation with quality scoring
